recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q1, 1:59: Shamet 3PT Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Brunson 4 AST). That single play moved CLE's chance of winning from 39% to 28%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went NYK's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Mitchell of CLE. Across 44 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 34 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q4, 3:47, with CLE down 45 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 0%. In other words: nearly impossible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q1, 10:22, with the game tilting CLE's way. The play in the middle of it: Mitchell 28' 3PT Pullup Jump Shot (6 PTS).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
